page.label: Focus areas
page.title: Curated Resources
page.lead: Maintained lists of the best tools, projects, models, and links across all local AI focus areas. Kept up to date as the space moves.

areas.label: Beginners
areas.title: Where to Start
areas.lead: New to local AI? The best beginner resources for understanding what local AI is, picking a model, and getting your first chat running on your own machine.

resources.name: Ollama
resources.description: The easiest way to get a local model running. Pull a model with one command and chat with it straight from the terminal or over a local API.

resources.name: Jan
resources.description: An open source, privacy-first desktop app for running local models, built as a ChatGPT alternative that works fully offline.

resources.name: LM Studio
resources.description: A polished desktop app for discovering, downloading, and chatting with local models. No command line required.

resources.name: GPT4All
resources.description: A free desktop app from Nomic AI for running local models, with a built-in LocalDocs feature for chatting with your own files completely offline.

resources.name: Open WebUI
resources.description: A self-hosted, feature-rich web interface for chatting with local models, built to run on top of Ollama or any OpenAI-compatible API.

resources.name: AnythingLLM
resources.description: An all-in-one desktop app for chatting with local models and your own documents, with built-in RAG, multi-user workspaces, and support for AI agents.

areas.label: Focus area
areas.title: Models
areas.lead: What is out there, who makes it, and where to find it. Links to HuggingFace, model makers, leaderboards, and anything worth knowing about the open model ecosystem.

resources.name: Hugging Face
resources.description: The largest hub for open models, datasets, and demos. Search and download almost any open model in GGUF, safetensors, or MLX format.

resources.name: Unsloth
resources.description: Day-one GGUF and 4-bit quantizations of nearly every major model release, plus the open source framework behind them for fine-tuning locally.

resources.name: MLX Community
resources.description: Pre-converted MLX-format models optimized to run natively and efficiently on Apple Silicon Macs.

resources.name: bartowski
resources.description: One of the most prolific and trusted community quantizers, with near-simultaneous GGUF releases for almost every notable new model.

resources.name: Ollama Library
resources.description: Browse and pull ready-to-run quantized models straight into Ollama. Not the full ecosystem, but the fastest path from browse to ollama run.

areas.label: Focus area
areas.title: Coding Harnesses
areas.lead: Open source tools for writing code with a local model. No API costs, no data sent anywhere. Links to projects, setup resources, and comparisons across the main options.

areas.label: Focus area
areas.title: Connect Applications
areas.lead: Tools and projects for connecting local models to other apps, workflows, and services. Run AI as a local API and plug it into the things you already use.

areas.label: Focus area
areas.title: Text to Speech and Speech to Text
areas.lead: Local voice AI. Transcribe audio with Whisper, generate natural speech with Kokoro and others, all running entirely on your own hardware.

areas.label: Focus area
areas.title: Home Labbing
areas.lead: Setting up a dedicated machine or multi-machine system at home. Run models as a local server, share access across your network, and build a proper local AI setup.
